channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. cruise passengers fly back for hantavirus monitoring, as one tests positive
item.description: Passengers are returning to their home countries after weeks on the ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. Most of the Americans aboard are headed for Nebraska to be evaluated by health officials.
item.content:encoded:
Passengers are returning to their home countries after weeks on the ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. Most of the Americans aboard are headed for Nebraska to be evaluated by health officials.

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Arizona students show love for their teachers
item.description: As students across the country celebrate teachers and school staff, some thoughtful learners in Arizona articulate why they appreciate theirs.
item.content:encoded: As students across the country celebrate teachers and school staff, some thoughtful learners in Arizona articulate why they appreciate theirs.
item.dc:creator: Noor Haghighi

item.title: Diving deep into the fascinating history of America's jaywalking laws
item.description: Jaywalking is often considered to be a pretty minor offense, but it is illegal in many American cities. KCUR's Mackenzie Martin offers a history of America's jaywalking laws.
item.content:encoded: Jaywalking is often considered to be a pretty minor offense, but it is illegal in many American cities. KCUR's Mackenzie Martin offers a history of America's jaywalking laws.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: Wisconsin residents hold 'caravans of love' to celebrate mothers in prison
item.description: A group of Wisconsinites organizes a "headlight caravan" outside a local women's prison. It's a show of solidarity for the women inside the prison to let them know they're not forgotten.
item.content:encoded: A group of Wisconsinites organizes a "headlight caravan" outside a local women's prison. It's a show of solidarity for the women inside the prison to let them know they're not forgotten.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lehr

item.title: Under the Trump administration, the State Department is seeing an exodus of diplomats
item.description: Under the Trump administration, the State Department is seeing an exodus of diplomats. Among the reasons: a newly aggressive politicization of U.S. foreign policy.
item.content:encoded: Under the Trump administration, the State Department is seeing an exodus of diplomats. Among the reasons: a newly aggressive politicization of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: A historian discusses Trump's plan to build an arch to commemorate 250 years
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with historian Kevin Levin about President Trump's proposed triumphal arch and how it would fit next other memorials in the nation's capital.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with historian Kevin Levin about President Trump's proposed triumphal arch and how it would fit next other memorials in the nation's capital.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Gas, groceries and getting by: How voters in one Ohio district see affordability
item.description: Ohio voters headed to the polls last week for primary elections, and in competitive districts like the one where Toledo is primarily located, the message was clear: affordability is a top priority.
item.content:encoded: Ohio voters headed to the polls last week for primary elections, and in competitive districts like the one where Toledo is primarily located, the message was clear: affordability is a top priority.
item.dc:creator: Catie Dull

item.title: What to know about the Canvas hack of student data
item.description: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.content:encoded: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Bobby Cox, Hall of Fame manager of Atlanta Braves, dies at age 84
item.description: The Braves announced Cox's death on Saturday. He managed the team to prominence during the 1990s and the team's only championship in 1995, before retiring after the 2010 season.
item.content:encoded:
The Braves announced Cox's death on Saturday. He managed the team to prominence during the 1990s and the team's only championship in 1995, before retiring after the 2010 season.

(Image credit: John Bazemore)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Passengers from virus-hit cruise ship fly home as CDC says outbreak threat is low
item.description: As hantavirus dominates the headlines, sparking fears of another debilitating pandemic, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has stressed that the risk of sweeping contagion is small.
item.content:encoded:
As hantavirus dominates the headlines, sparking fears of another debilitating pandemic, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has stressed that the risk of sweeping contagion is small.

(Image credit: Manu Fernandez)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
